headlineSummary: Google Gemini had limited evidence but the available mention was positive, while Anthropic and OpenAI were mixed overall with both praise and criticism in the discussion [E5][E1][E4][E7].

ranking.summary: OpenAI came through as mixed, with one commenter expressing enthusiasm for Codex but another criticizing OpenAI account behavior [E3][E7].
Anthropic’s discussion was mixed: some commenters praised Claude Code as inspiring and useful, while others said Claude felt unfulfilling or objected to Anthropic’s behavior [E1][E2][E4][E6][E7].
Google Gemini received limited evidence, but the cited comment was positive and said Claude & Gemini helped with coding and made the experience a great time [E5].

evidence.excerpt: Tell HN: I'm 60 years old. Claude Code has re-ignited a passion

annotations.rationale: The title explicitly names Claude Code and says it 'has re-ignited a passion,' which is strong praise for the product's impact.

evidence.excerpt: Tools like Claude Code are the ultimate cheat code for me and have breathed new life into my desire to create. I know more than enough about architecture and coding to understand the plumbing and effectively debug, yet I don't have to know or care about implementation details. It's almost an unfair unlock.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly praises Claude Code as an 'ultimate cheat code' that 'breathed new life' into their desire to create.

evidence.excerpt: I was so shocked when I found out that I could experience that feeling again with Claude Code and Codex. I guess it was like experiencing your first love all over again? I slept late, I woke up early, I couldn't wait to go back to my Codex and Claude.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly names Claude Code and describes eagerly returning to 'my Codex and Claude' like 'first love all over again,' which is strong praise.
The commenter explicitly names Codex and says they 'couldn't wait to go back' to it, indicating strong enthusiasm for the OpenAI tool.

evidence.excerpt: I spent the last 2 days primarily using Claude instead of coding things myself at work. I felt the exact opposite way. It was so unfulfilling. I’d equate it to the feeling of getting an A on a test, knowing I cheated. I didn’t accomplish anything. I didn’t learn anything. I got the end result with none of the satisfaction and learned nothing in the process.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says using Claude was 'so unfulfilling' and that they 'didn’t learn anything,' which is strong criticism of the experience.

evidence.excerpt: But I'm coding now, with the help of Claude & Gemini, and having a great time. Each block of Python or Applescript that they generate for me is a much better learning tool than a book - I'm going through the code line by line and researching everything. And I'm also learning how to deal with LLMs and their strengths & weaknesses.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly credits Claude with helping them code, says they are 'having a great time,' and calls its output a better learning tool than a book.
The commenter explicitly credits Gemini with helping them code, says they are 'having a great time,' and calls its output a better learning tool than a book.

evidence.excerpt: Claude normally gets it right (though sometimes horribly wrong) - this is easier to catch in autocomplete

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly references Claude and gives mixed quality feedback: it 'normally gets it right' but is also 'sometimes horribly wrong.'

evidence.excerpt: This is a great reminder that the line drawn by Anthropic is already too far and that if you’ve been driven to cancel your OpenAI account by their behavior you should also cancel your Anthropic account.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says Anthropic's line is 'already too far' and urges canceling an Anthropic account, which is strong disapproval of Anthropic's conduct.
The commenter explicitly says people may have been 'driven to cancel' their OpenAI account 'by their behavior,' which is strong negative sentiment toward OpenAI.
